title: coreopsis californica
intro: Coreopsis californica, also known as California tickseed, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The medicinal properties of Coreopsis californica have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections, due to its ability to soothe and calm the body. In herbal preparations, Coreopsis californica is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
